CHN.name: China
CHN.story: The undisputed giant: about half of all new cars sold in China are now electric, and Chinese brands like BYD sell more EVs than anyone on earth.

USA.name: United States
USA.story: A patchwork nation: California looks like Europe while other states barely register — the US average hides a 10x gap between its states.

VNM.name: Vietnam
VNM.story: Home-grown VinFast went all-electric and flooded the streets with e-scooters, making Vietnam one of Asia's fastest two-wheeler switchers.

DEU.name: Germany
DEU.story: Europe's car-making heartland is going electric under pressure from EU CO2 rules, though subsidy cuts cooled demand in 2024.

IND.name: India
IND.story: India electrifies the small stuff first: more than half of its new three-wheelers (the ubiquitous auto-rickshaw) are now electric, even as EV cars stay niche.

NOR.name: Norway
NOR.story: The finish line, almost reached. Norway taxed gas cars hard and rewarded EVs for years — now ~9 in 10 new cars are electric.

NPL.name: Nepal
NPL.story: Nepal runs on hydropower — nearly its entire grid is clean — and slashed EV import taxes, so battery cars now make up a striking share of its imports.

BRA.name: Brazil
BRA.story: Chinese brands arrived fast; BYD and GWM turned Brazil into Latin America's busiest EV market almost overnight.

THA.name: Thailand
THA.story: Thailand offered cash incentives and became Southeast Asia's EV assembly hub, with Chinese automakers building factories there.
